lines.text: Tucker Traywick, body-cam relay from the Vilas Park perimeter. Madison-wide count as of {{CLOCK}} is 139 confirmed kills. That's 17 in the last hour — down slightly from the 22 we saw 01:00 to 02:00 last year. We're tracking {{ELAPSED}} into tonight.

East Side is still pulling heavy. Atwood corridor alone accounts for 28 of our 139 — that's 20.1% of total activity. Holden, you were running the weapons-trade pattern earlier. The bladed-versus-firearm ratio in that zone is running inverted this year.

Position moving north toward the Mendota shoreline now. Water temperature holding at 28 degrees. Visibility degraded — the fog off the lake is thicker than Caroline's 05:00 forecast predicted. That's going to suppress activity in the water-exposure zones.

Capitol Square perimeter remains fortified. No breach attempts since the 21:40 incident. I'm counting eight private security contractors on the south facade. Their presence is — wait, movement, someone's — I need to relocate, there's a group moving east on Gorham Street, I'm going to —

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 139 (17 this hour, -5 YoY vs. 2168 hour 8)
East Side (Atwood/Schenk's): 28 kills, 20.1% of total — sustained lead
Lake Mendota fog advisory — visibility 1.5 miles. Water exposure risk suppressed.
PurgeShield perimeter-check: Your panels holding? Verify seals at {{CLOCK}}. 1-800-SAFE-NOW.
